title: save_key_package_capped and count_and_create_invoice use manual BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT / ROLLBACK instead of sqlx pool.begin()
description: relay/src/store.rs has three functions (save_key_package_capped, enqueue, count_and_create_invoice) that use pool.acquire() + raw sqlx::query('BEGIN IMMEDIATE') + manual COMMIT/ROLLBACK. sqlx does not natively support BEGIN IMMEDIATE via pool.begin(), but the pattern of matching on result.is_ok() to decide COMMIT vs ROLLBACK is error-prone: if result is Ok but COMMIT fails, the ROLLBACK in the else branch is never reached (there is no else branch for COMMIT failure). Rewrite: after acquiring the conn, set PRAGMA locking_mode=EXCLUSIVE on the connection, then use pool.begin() for the standard sqlx transaction API. Alternatively: accept the current approach but add a comment on why COMMIT failure is not handled and what the DB state would be.
